questions.q: In which year was the Prabhat Film Company founded?
questions.explanation: Prabhat Film Company was founded in 1929 by V. Shantaram and his partners.

questions.q: Which legendary filmmaker was a co-founder of the Prabhat Film Company?
questions.explanation: V. Shantaram was a leading co-founder of Prabhat Film Company and its most celebrated director.

questions.q: To which city did the Prabhat Film Company relocate in 1933?
questions.explanation: Prabhat moved its operations from Kolhapur to Pune in 1933, where it built a large studio campus.

questions.q: The 1936 Prabhat film Sant Tukaram was based on the life of a saint from which devotional tradition?
questions.explanation: Sant Tukaram depicted the life of the 17th-century Marathi Varkari poet-saint Tukaram.

questions.q: At which international film festival was Prabhat's Sant Tukaram honoured in 1937?
questions.explanation: Sant Tukaram was recognised at the Venice Film Festival in 1937, a landmark for Indian cinema.

questions.q: The former Pune campus of the Prabhat Film Company later became the grounds of which national institution?
options: The Film and Television Institute of India (FTII)
questions.explanation: Prabhat's Pune studio site later became the campus of the Film and Television Institute of India (FTII).

questions.q: Prabhat Film Company was best known for films of which themes?
questions.explanation: Prabhat was renowned for socially reformist and devotional films rather than escapist genres.

questions.q: Which 1935 Prabhat film directed by V. Shantaram tackled the social evil of untouchability?
questions.explanation: Dharmatma (1935), V. Shantaram's biopic of the saint Eknath, confronted untouchability and was a hallmark of Prabhat's reformist cinema.

questions.q: The name 'Prabhat' chosen for the film company means what in English?
questions.explanation: The word 'Prabhat' means 'dawn' or 'morning', reflecting the studio's hopeful, reformist outlook.

questions.q: In which decade did the Prabhat Film Company eventually cease operations?
questions.explanation: Prabhat Film Company wound down and stopped producing films in the early 1950s.

questions.q: In which city was the Prabhat Film Company originally established in 1929 before its move to Pune?
questions.explanation: Prabhat was first set up in Kolhapur in 1929 and relocated to Pune in 1933.

questions.q: Which co-founder of Prabhat, known for sound recording and cinematography, was among V. Shantaram's key early partners?
questions.explanation: Sheikh Fattelal, along with Vishnupant Damle, K. R. Dhaibar and S. B. Kulkarni, co-founded Prabhat with V. Shantaram.

questions.q: The 1937 Prabhat film Duniya Na Mane (Kunku), about a young woman married to a much older man, was directed by whom?
questions.explanation: Duniya Na Mane, a reformist drama on mismatched marriage, was directed by V. Shantaram for Prabhat.

questions.q: Sant Tukaram (1936), one of Prabhat's most famous films, was primarily made in which language?
questions.explanation: Sant Tukaram was made in Marathi, drawing on the Varkari devotional tradition of Maharashtra.

questions.q: Which 1933 Prabhat film is regarded as India's first colour film, processed abroad?
questions.explanation: Sairandhri (1933) is often cited as India's first colour film, though its colour processing done in Germany disappointed Prabhat.

questions.q: Prabhat's first sound film, released in 1932 and telling the story of the truthful King Harishchandra, was titled what?
questions.explanation: Ayodhyecha Raja (1932), based on the legend of King Harishchandra, was Prabhat's first talkie and one of the earliest Marathi sound films.

questions.q: The well-known music director who began his career at the Prabhat Film Company before becoming a giant of Hindi film music was?
questions.explanation: Keshavrao Bhole was Prabhat's noted music director, scoring films such as Duniya Na Mane and Amar Jyoti.

questions.q: Which future legendary singer-actor worked as an employee at the Prabhat studio in Pune early in his career?
questions.explanation: Sudhir Phadke (Babuji) was associated with Prabhat early on, and the studio nurtured several future music and film talents.

questions.q: Prabhat's 1939 bilingual film Aadmi (Manoos) sympathetically portrayed the life of which character?
options: A prostitute and a policeman who loves her
questions.explanation: Aadmi (Manoos) told a compassionate story of a policeman in love with a prostitute, a bold social theme for its time.

questions.q: V. Shantaram eventually left Prabhat in the early 1940s to found which of his own production companies?
questions.explanation: V. Shantaram left Prabhat in 1942 to establish Rajkamal Kalamandir in Mumbai.

questions.q: Which 1934 Prabhat film directed by V. Shantaram was its first production made in Pune and a landmark of its early sound era?
questions.explanation: Amrit Manthan (1934) was Prabhat's first film produced after its move to Pune and an acclaimed early sound film directed by V. Shantaram.

questions.q: Prabhat's 1941 film Shejari (Padosi) is remembered for promoting which message?
questions.explanation: Shejari (Padosi) carried a powerful message of Hindu-Muslim unity, fitting Prabhat's socially conscious ethos.

questions.q: Who played the title role of the saint in Prabhat's Sant Tukaram (1936)?
questions.explanation: Vishnupant Pagnis gave a celebrated performance as Tukaram, becoming closely identified with the saintly role.

questions.q: Sant Tukaram (1936) is often noted as one of the first Indian films to achieve which distinction?
options: Running continuously for over a year in one theatre
questions.explanation: Sant Tukaram reportedly ran for more than a year at a single Mumbai cinema, a rare feat for its era.

questions.q: Which co-director duo directed Prabhat's Sant Tukaram (1936)?
options: Baburao Painter and K. R. Dhaibar
Debaki Bose and P. C. Barua
questions.explanation: Sant Tukaram was directed by the Prabhat partners Vishnupant Damle and Sheikh Fattelal.

questions.q: Before founding Prabhat, V. Shantaram had worked at which earlier Kolhapur-based film studio run by Baburao Painter?
questions.explanation: V. Shantaram and several Prabhat founders trained at Baburao Painter's Maharashtra Film Company in Kolhapur.

questions.q: Prabhat's 1944 historical film Ramshastri portrayed the upright chief justice of which dynasty's court?
options: The Peshwas of the Maratha Empire
questions.explanation: Ramshastri (1944) depicted the principled judge Ram Shastri Prabhune of the Peshwa court in Pune.

questions.q: The English-titled international version of Prabhat's Duniya Na Mane was released under which name?
questions.explanation: Duniya Na Mane was screened internationally under the English title 'The Unexpected'.

questions.q: Which actor, later a renowned filmmaker, made his screen debut as a young extra at the Prabhat studio in Pune?
questions.explanation: Guru Dutt worked at Prabhat in the early 1940s, where he also befriended Dev Anand, before becoming a major filmmaker.

questions.q: Prabhat's reformist 1936 Marathi film Amar Jyoti, screened at Venice, centred on a strong female protagonist who became what?
options: A pirate captain rebelling against an unjust law
questions.explanation: Amar Jyoti (1936) featured a woman who turns pirate to fight oppressive patriarchal law, reflecting Prabhat's progressive themes.
